channel.title: Old Books, New Attitudes
channel.description: Recent content on Old Books, New Attitudes

item.title: Introduction
item.description: The Project Historians of medicine and science agree that something pivotal happened in England in the latter decades of the sixteenth century, as medieval theories about the body or the natural world diminished in the face of new discoveries, new ideas, and a new experimental method. And yet, the reading habits of sixteenth-century English artisans, bureaucrats, merchants, and farmers tell a different story, one that this project seeks to explore. Early modern English people were avid collectors of medieval manuscripts filled with centuries-old texts related to medicine, astrology, agriculture, or craft manufacture.

item.title: About
item.description: Developing the Edition and Site This site was developed on a Hugo template hosted on GitHub pages. The linked repository contains the basic content for the site: the Introduction, About, and Credit pages. The data and metadata generated from the manuscript transcriptions and annotations are held in a separate GitHub repository, technologies-of-history/dyngley-data. The two repositories are linked to one another with EditionCrafter, a publication tool for digital critical editions developed by the Making and Knowing Project (M&K) and Performant Software Solutions, with funding from the National Science Foundation.

item.title: Credits
item.description: Lead Editor Melissa Reynolds, Assistant Professor of History, Texas Christian University Student Researchers Student Folio Transcribed Jane Allinger TCC MS O.8.35 f. 64r Fiona Corrigan TCC MS O.8.35 f. 64v Camila Erazo TCC MS O.8.35 f. 65r Hudson Hahn TCC MS O.8.35 f. 65v Stella Lenzie TCC MS O.8.35 f. 66r Kasia Love TCC MS O.8.35 f. 66v Jonathan Martinez TCC MS O.8.35 f. 67r Kylie Millar TCC MS O.8.35 f.
